title: Public opinion dynamics regarding the restart of Japanese nuclear power plants following the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi disaster

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on Asahi Shimbun June 2012 polling trends post-Ohi restart announcement
Estimated: NHK polling data following Ohi reactor restart
Estimated: Interpolated trend between summer and winter 2012
Estimated: Gradual stabilization of energy policy discourse

events.description: Prime Minister Yoshihiko Noda announces the restart of Ohi nuclear reactors 3 and 4.
Ohi reactor 3 becomes the first to restart since the Fukushima disaster.
LDP wins landslide victory in general election, signaling a shift toward pro-restart policy.

agents.name: Liberal Democratic Party (LDP)
agents.description: Pro-nuclear energy policy advocates seeking economic stability.

agents.name: Democratic Party of Japan (DPJ)
agents.description: Divided party leadership struggling with post-Fukushima energy strategy.

agents.name: METI (Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry)
agents.description: Government body pushing for nuclear restart to ensure energy security.

agents.name: Anti-Nuclear Protest Movement
agents.description: Grassroots groups organizing large-scale protests in Tokyo.

agents.name: Nuclear Regulation Authority (NRA)
agents.description: New independent regulator established to oversee safety standards.

agents.name: General Public (Urban)
agents.description: Concerned citizens balancing energy costs with safety fears.

notes: Pro-side is defined as respondents who support the restart of nuclear power plants. Against-side is defined as respondents who oppose the restart. Data quality: Early rounds are estimated based on historical trends from major Japanese newspapers; final round is based on verified survey data.
